items.title: Elizabeth Farrant at Women in Life Sciences networking breakfast, Cambridge
items.body: Dr Elizabeth Farrant attended the Women in Life Sciences networking breakfast at Chesterford Research Park, Cambridge. The event featured a session on patent strategy from the perspective of in-house counsel, including insight from Sally Curran on how pharma diligences therapeutics IP. This is directly relevant as PDC-1 advances towards the clinic.

items.title: Elizabeth Farrant presents in vivo efficacy and toxicity results at BioBabraham Symposium
items.body: Dr Elizabeth Farrant presented New Path Molecular's latest in vivo efficacy and toxicity data at the BioBabraham Symposium on 26 June 2026, marking the company's first public presentation of preclinical results for PDC-1.

items.title: PDC-1 shows no toxicity in pathology study
items.body: A pathology assessment comparing PDC-1 against vehicle control demonstrated no detectable toxicity, providing an early and encouraging safety signal as the programme advances towards IND-enabling studies.

items.title: New Path Molecular awarded £800k Innovate UK Biomedical Catalyst Grant
items.body: New Path Molecular has been awarded an £800,000 Innovate UK Biomedical Catalyst Grant — comprising £560,000 in grant funding and £240,000 in matched funding secured by the company — to advance the development of PDC-1. The award represents independent peer-reviewed validation of the programme's scientific and commercial potential.

items.title: New Path Molecular receives £100k Innovate UK Feasibility Study grant
items.body: New Path Molecular was awarded a £100,000 Innovate UK Feasibility Study grant, providing the initial non-dilutive funding to validate the phosphatidylserine-targeting approach and establish the scientific foundation for the PDC-1 programme.
